title: Armor Durability Numbers (1.8-1.21+)
description: See the armor durability state of every armor piece: leather, chainmail, iron, gold, diamond, and netherite.
body:
## What is this?
This resource pack displays the **exact durability** of every armor piece in Minecraft.
Including leather, chainmail, iron, gold, diamond, and netherite armor.

![Image shows all the armor types](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/3a88bf670789972721cecd75f30c98e386341575.png)


## How it works?
### Option 1 – Fabric users 1.21.1 and below
**Requires**: [Cit Resewn Mod](https://modrinth.com/mod/cit-resewn)

1. Install CIT Resewn into your mods folder.

2. Place the resource pack into your resourcepacks folder and enable it.
   
### Option 2 – Forge or Vanilla users
**Requires**: [OptiFine](https://optifine.net/downloads)

1. Install OptiFine.
   
2. In-game, go to Video Settings → Quality → Enable Custom Items.

4. Place the resource pack into your resourcepacks folder and enable it.

### Option 3 – fabric 1.21.4+ users and above 

I made a resource pack for people playing on fabric 1.21.4-1.21.11.

Go check it out!
- https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/visible-durability



## Reported Bugs
- Doesn't work on Fabric if you are using OptiFine. You have to use CIT resewn. For the fps I recommend [sodium](https://modrinth.com/mod/sodium).
- Some armor pieces, like gold, display an incorrect number near 100 durability. (If you know a fix, contact me on Discord and I’ll update the pack. 😀)

## Allowed on Servers?
Me? Breaking rules? Never 😇 …but uhh, maybe check with the mods just in case 👀
